body_txt: A nice side effect of acting is that you don't have to play just one character, but if a production likes you enough a guest actor can return to play more than one character in the same show. The use of the same actor for different characters is typically intended to go unnoticed or at least unremarked. Same with The Other Darrin , everyone is just trying to move on like everything is normal. This is especially common in sci-fi and fantasy shows partially because the ability to act while playing a Rubber-Forehead Alien is its' own talent, and the production already has scans, casts and molds of the actor on file. They may also have just developed a good rapport with the cast and crew , endearing them for further returns despite the fact they may die every episode . The casual way this was once done has fallen out of favor as Season Fluidity becomes more pronounced and fans are actively confused seeing the same actor in two different roles breaking the story immersion. Acting for Two is related but is directed towards multiple roles in the same installment, and can apply to such things as a series regular having to portray an imposter with Magic Plastic Surgery . They can and have overlapped. Recast as a Regular is when a recurring actor gets recruited to play a main character while ignoring the bit role they had before. This has sometimes had an impact on fan discussions. Babylon 5 was so intricately plotted that, when Ed Wasser played Shadow minion Morden, fans wondered if his role in the series pilot as a character credited as Guerra meant the Shadows were already influencing events back then. J. Michael Straczynski said they just liked the actor's work in the pilot and cast him in a recurring role. Sharp-eyed fans may assume such things anyway , especially if one particular performance was a small bit that can easily justify a name change or personality change when brought in for a major role. This is such a common occurrence amongst the various incarnations of Law & Order that fans of that show have come to call those who look familiar "Repeat Offenders" . When it is done intentionally it typically results in an Identical Stranger , Identical Grandson , or Uncanny Family Resemblance . Compare Celebrity Paradox , You Might Remember Me from... and Voice-Only Cameo . Contrast with The Other Darrin . Can be Handwaved via Direct Line to the Author . You ALL Look Familiar is not related unless character sprites and models can be considered "guest actors".
